metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add grep-based proving commands for each content AC (e.g., `grep -c 'Two writers, one store' docs/architecture.md`, `grep -E 'applier\._apply_batch' docs/architecture.md`, `grep -rn 'Two writers, one store' src/rebar/_store/event_append.py src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py`) so every AC has at least one concrete, executable verification step.
findings.finding: The content-bearing ACs (what the section must say, what the docstrings must say) have no grep-based or structural proving commands; `make lint` and `make typecheck` verify syntax only, not content.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a '## Verification' block to the plan with commands such as:
```
grep -q 'Two writers, one store' docs/architecture.md
grep -q 'applier._apply_batch' docs/architecture.md
grep -q '_write_event_file' docs/architecture.md
grep -q 'reconcile.*apply.*batch.*sync' docs/architecture.md  # vocabulary list
grep -rq 'Two writers, one store' src/rebar/_store/event_append.py
grep -rq 'Two writers, one store' src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py
grep -rq 'Two writers, one store' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py
make lint && make typecheck
```
Each exit-0 means the corresponding AC is satisfied.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a docstring signpost in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` (specifically on or near `_apply_batch`) pointing to the new 'Two writers, one store' section, consistent with the plan's stated goal of fixing confusion where an agent greps.
findings.finding: The plan identifies `applier._apply_batch` as the primary false-friend causing agent confusion, but does NOT add a docstring signpost to `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` — the exact file an agent lands on when grepping for 'batch'. The three signposted files (`event_append.py`, `import_ndjson.py`, `inbound_translate.py`) are the correct write-path files, but miss the false-friend module itself.
findings.location: Scope section — docstring signpost list; also AC lines 6–8
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: '- [ ] A docstring cross-ref to the section is added in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py`, noting that `_apply_batch` is an OUTBOUND Jira REST sequencer (not a local commit-batcher), with a pointer to docs/architecture.md#two-writers-one-store.' Add this to the Scope bullet listing the signpost files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cross-reference docstring signpost to `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` (the actual false-friend landing site named in 'Why') pointing to the 'Two writers, one store' section, or explicitly justify in Notes why the confusion site itself is intentionally excluded.
findings.finding: The plan mandates docstring signposts on the LOCAL write-path side (`event_append.py`, `import_ndjson.py`, `inbound_translate.py`) but omits a signpost in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` — the exact file where confused agents arrive when grepping 'batch'/'commit'. The plan's own 'Why' section names `applier._apply_batch` as the primary false friend causing agent confusion, yet the Notes section only explains dropping the `__main__.py` signpost (wrong site, wrong rationale) without addressing why `applier.py` itself is excluded. This is a textbook instruction-locality antipattern: the correction is placed away from the confusion site.
findings.location: Scope / Notes sections; AC items for docstring cross-refs
findings.suggested_fix: In the Notes section, either (a) add `applier.py` to the list of files receiving a docstring signpost (one line at the top of the existing module docstring: `# See docs/architecture.md § 'Two writers, one store' — _apply_batch is an OUTBOUND Jira REST sequencer.`) and add a corresponding AC line, OR (b) add an explicit rationale explaining why the false-friend file itself is intentionally excluded (e.g., 'the existing applier.py docstring already says "outbound-batch *sequencer*" at line 2, which is sufficient').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `__main__.py` scope-exclusion note by naming the specific file: replace 'the `__main__.py` signpost' with 'the `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py` signpost' (or `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`) so the exclusion rationale is traceable to the correct file.
findings.finding: The scope-exclusion note says 'the only `commit_tree`/`update_ref` there is the legacy-lock-file purge' but `src/rebar/__main__.py` contains no `commit_tree` or `update_ref` at all — it is a 2-line CLI dispatcher. The `commit_tree`/`update_ref` usage that the note actually refers to lives in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`. The ambiguous 'there' could mislead a future maintainer who tries to verify or update the exclusion rationale.
findings.location: Notes section — '`__main__.py` signpost from the original plan was intentionally dropped'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Notes section from: 'The `__main__.py` signpost from the original plan was intentionally dropped: the only `commit_tree`/`update_ref` there is the legacy-lock-file purge…' to: 'The `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py` (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`) signpost from the original plan was intentionally dropped: the only `commit_tree`/`update_ref` there is the legacy-lock-file purge (orphan-branch CAS commit), not an event-file batch commit…'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
